content.full_text:
PLEASE REPLY TO
D11 11TH STREET
SACRAMENTO CA 95814
(916) 427-0163

POST OFFICE BOX 2301
SANTA ROSA CA 95405

2641 LUNA VISTA ROAD
VENTURA CA 93001

P.O. BOX 2308 EVAMP
BAY 248
WOOD AND HILLS CA 91364

COMMITTEES

CHAIRMAN
EDUCATION COMMITTEE
SUBCOMMITTEE ON EDUCATION
AND DRUG ENFORCEMENT

VICE-CHAIRMAN
JOINT COMMITTEE FOR REVISION OF
THE PENAL CODE

STANDING COMMITTEES
EDUCATION
ENERGY AND PUBLIC UTILITIES
LOCAL GOVERNMENT AND FINANCE
TRANSPORTATION
TAXATION AND PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION

SELECT COMMITTEES
DRUG AND ALCOHOL ABUSE

California State Senate

GARY K. HART
SENATOR
EIGHTEENTH DISTRICT

CHAIRMAN
EDUCATION COMMITTEE

March 8, 1990


Jon Rothman
Legal Division
FPPC
428 J Street  #800
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Mr. Rothman:

I am writing following our phone conversation today. The
following explains the situation relating to the event planned
for April 1st and supersedes my prior letter dated February 21,
1990. I am still requesting a written opinion from your office.

A constituent approached Senator Hart and offered to have his
family host a fundraising event to benefit the Senator's
re-election campaign. This family would like to hold this event
at a ranch in the Santa Monica Mountains which is often rented
for events. There is absolutely no relationship between the
ranch and the Senator's campaign; the family has both personal
and business relationships with the ranch owner and has hosted
charitable events with his help in the past. If the ranch owner
enters into a contract with our constituent family and the family
then hosts the event, is the campaign contribution from the
family?

It is my understanding that the ranch owner is intending to
charge approximately $8.50 per person for the menu selected and
not charge for the use of his facility. (His posted price list
includes $8.00 per person charge for use of the facility.) It
is because of the ranch owner's relationship with this family
that he is willing to provide both the food and facility at a
total charge of $8.50 per person charge for use of the facility. Under these circumstances, is
the family hosting this event making a campaign contribution? If
it is determined that they are, what is this contribution
because the ranch owner and the family are entering into a
contract for the use of the facilities, is the ranch owner also
making a contribution?

NOT PRINTED AT GOVERNMENT EXPENSE
Courtesy of Gary Hart of California

MAR  8 '90 15:46        818 704 035  PAGE.033

Legal Division
FPPC
March 8, 1990
Page 2


On a slightly different subject, in our conversation on Friday,
February 16th, you informed me that if the ranch owner is the
controlling director of the corporation, there is only one
contribution possible from both the owner and the corporation.
However, if other owners or directors wished to make
contributions, they could do so. For instance, if the ranch
owner's wife or mother had an interest in the business they could
make "in kind" contributions to the campaign. Susan Soja,
Director of Sales for Calamigos Ranch, informed me that Glen
Gerson is the person who controls and directs the operations of
the corporation; he has the final say on all decisions. His
mother and father are directors whose function is to bring
business to the ranch. Mr. Gerson's wife is not on the Board;
however, her name is on loan papers for the corporation; she
co-signs on loans.

If you need additional information or clarification regarding my
questions, or if I misunderstood Friday's conversation in any
way, please do not hesitate to contact me. Please respond to me
by mail at 4260 Old Topanga Canyon Road; I can be reached by
phone at home at (818)703-1420.

Thank you very much for your prompt attention to this.

                                        sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Elaine Miller

HART** SENATOR EIGHTEENTH DISTRICT CHAIRMAN EDUCATION COMMITTEE March 8, 1990 Jon Rothman Legal Division FPPC 428 J Street #800 Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Mr. Rothman: I am writing following our phone conversation today. The following explains the situation relating to the event planned for April 1st and supersedes my prior letter dated February 21, 1990. I am still requesting a written opinion from your office. A constituent approached Senator Hart and offered to have his family host a fundraising event to benefit the Senator's re-election campaign. This family would like to hold this event at a ranch in the Santa Monica Mountains which is often rented for events. There is absolutely no relationship between the ranch and the Senator's campaign; the family has both personal and business relationships with the ranch owner and has hosted charitable events with his help in the past. If the ranch owner enters into a contract with our constituent family and the family then hosts the event, is the campaign contribution from the family? It is my understanding that the ranch owner is intending to charge approximately $8.50 per person for the menu selected, and not charge for the use of his facility. (His posted price list includes an $8.00 per person charge for use of the facility.) It is because of the ranch owner's relationship with this family, that he is willing to provide both the food and facility at a total charge of $8.50 per person. Under these circumstances, is the family hosting this event making a campaign contribution? If it is determined that they are, what is this contribution? Because the ranch owner and the family are entering into a contract for the use of the facilities, is the ranch owner also making a contribution? On a slightly different subject, in our conversation on Friday, February 16th, you informed me that if the ranch owner is the controlling director of the corporation, there is only one contribution possible from both the owner and the corporation. However, if other owners or directors wished to make contributions, they could do so. For instance, if the ranch owner's wife or mother had an interest in the business they could also make "in kind" contributions to the campaign. Susan Soja, Director of Sales for Calamigos Ranch, informed me that Glen Gerson is the person who controls and directs the operations of the corporation; he has the final say on all decisions. His mother and father are directors whose function is to bring business to the ranch. Mr. Gerson's wife is not on the Board; however, her name is on loan papers for the corporation; she co-signs on loans. If you need additional information or clarification regarding my questions, or if I misunderstood Friday's conversation in any way, please do not hesitate to contact me.
